original_text:
### Iconography
- Style: **minimal line illustration** for clarity and quick recall. New icons are permitted but **must match this stylistic approach** (consistent stroke weight, rounded line caps, single-color line work).
- Icon colors and backgrounds adjustable **only within the brand palette**.
- Consider scale and contrast for low-vision audiences. [GENERAL: minimum render size 32×32px in email; default Brand Blue strokes on light backgrounds.]

relations.note: The email accessibility requirements refine the general iconography treatment for email use on light backgrounds.

quotes: New icons are permitted but **must match this stylistic approach** (consistent stroke weight, rounded line caps, single-color line work).
Icon colors and backgrounds adjustable **only within the brand palette**.

rules.intent: Maintain a clear, recognizable, and consistently branded icon system.
rules.rule_text: Icons use the minimal line illustration style (minimal single-color line work with rounded line caps); any new icons must match this stylistic approach, including consistent stroke weight. Icon colors and backgrounds may be adjusted only within the brand palette.

quotes: minimum render size 32×32px in email; default Brand Blue strokes on light backgrounds.
Consider scale and contrast for low-vision audiences. [GENERAL: minimum render size 32×32px in email; default Brand Blue strokes on light backgrounds.]

rules.intent: Keep email icons legible and sufficiently contrasted for low-vision audiences.
rules.rule_text: For low-vision accessibility in email, icons must render at least 32×32px; on light backgrounds, default icon strokes use Brand Blue (#00529b). Consider scale and contrast for low-vision audiences.
